subject: Civics
title: Role of the Government in Health

questions.text: Health refers to our ability to remain free from:
questions.explanation: Health is not just about diseases; it also involves mental well-being and a clean environment.

questions.text: Apart from disease, which of the following is an important factor for health?
questions.explanation: If people live in unhygienic conditions or don't get enough food, they are more likely to fall ill.

questions.text: India has the largest number of ________ in the world.
questions.explanation: India produces a high number of doctors every year, yet healthcare access remains a challenge for many.

questions.text: What is the primary reason why many people in India do not receive proper healthcare?
questions.explanation: Public hospitals are overcrowded, and private hospitals are too expensive for the poor.

questions.text: Public health services are a chain of health centres and hospitals run by the:
questions.explanation: These services are funded by the taxes we pay to the government.

questions.text: At the village level, health services are usually provided by a:
questions.explanation: They are trained to deal with common illnesses and work under the supervision of doctors at Primary Health Centres (PHC).

questions.text: PHC stands for:
questions.explanation: PHCs cover several villages in a rural area and are the first point of contact for public healthcare.

questions.text: Why are public health services called 'public'?
options: They are located in public places
questions.explanation: The government is committed to providing healthcare to all citizens using the resources contributed by the people.

questions.text: Which of the following is a key feature of private health facilities?
options: They are owned and managed by private individuals/companies
They have long queues like public hospitals
questions.explanation: Private facilities are often expensive and motivated by profit.

questions.text: In private hospitals, patients have to pay ________ for every service.
questions.explanation: Private healthcare is often unaffordable for a large section of the population.

questions.text: According to our Constitution, it is the primary duty of the government to ensure the ________ of the people.
questions.explanation: Providing healthcare is an essential part of the government's responsibility towards its citizens.

questions.text: The Right to ________ is a part of the Right to Life guaranteed by the Constitution.
questions.explanation: If the government cannot provide timely medical treatment, it means the protection of life is not being ensured.

questions.text: Ranjan, a boy from a well-to-do family, went to a ________ hospital for treatment.
questions.explanation: In the story, he received quick and clean service but had to pay a high fee.

questions.text: Aman, who came from a poor background, had to wait in long queues at the ________ hospital.
questions.explanation: The story highlights the contrast between public and private healthcare experiences.

questions.text: In India, about ________ per cent of the population can afford the medicines required during an illness.
questions.explanation: This shows that for the majority of Indians, even basic medical costs are a burden.

questions.text: For the poor, every illness in the family is a cause of great:
questions.explanation: Illness leads to loss of wages and high medical expenses, pushing them deeper into poverty.

questions.text: Women in many parts of India are ________ to see a doctor compared to men.
questions.explanation: Gender inequality often results in women's health being considered less important than men's.

questions.text: In 1996, which Indian state made a major change by giving 40% of its budget to Panchayats?
questions.explanation: This allowed village councils to plan and provide for water, food, and education, improving health.

questions.text: Which country is considered to be one of the healthiest in Central America?
questions.explanation: Costa Rica decided not to have an army and used the money for healthcare, education, and basic needs.

questions.text: Costa Rica believes that a country has to be ________ for its development.
questions.explanation: A healthy population is essential for a nation's growth and progress.

questions.text: Medical tourism in India is growing because:
options: Hospitals provide world-class facilities at lower costs
questions.explanation: People from other countries come to India for surgeries and treatments that are cheaper than in their home countries.

questions.text: A 'Communicable Disease' is one that is:
options: Spread through water, food, air, or contact
questions.explanation: Examples include diarrhea, worms, and hepatitis.

questions.text: Public health services also work to prevent the spread of diseases like:
questions.explanation: Government organizes campaigns for vaccination and mosquito control.

questions.text: The government uses ________ to provide various public services for the benefit of all.
questions.explanation: Taxes fund the defense, police, judicial system, and healthcare.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a private health facility?
questions.explanation: PHCs are run by the government.

questions.text: In many private hospitals, doctors may prescribe ________ medicines just for profit.
questions.explanation: Ethical concerns often arise in private healthcare due to the profit motive.

questions.text: Poor people are often ________ before they even fall ill.
questions.explanation: Lack of adequate food makes them vulnerable to diseases.

questions.text: To improve health, it is necessary to work on ________ as well as ________.
questions.explanation: Good health requires both medical facilities and a clean, safe environment.

questions.text: RMP stands for:
questions.explanation: They are private doctors practicing in rural areas.

questions.text: Public hospitals have the responsibility to provide ________ to everyone in an emergency.
questions.explanation: Emergency care is a vital function of the public healthcare system.

questions.text: What does a government hospital require for people to stand in?
questions.explanation: Due to high demand and limited staff, queues are common in government hospitals.

questions.text: Health is about more than just ________.
questions.explanation: It's a state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being.

questions.text: Many tribal areas have ________ health centres and they don't run properly.
questions.explanation: Geographical and social barriers often limit healthcare in tribal regions.

questions.text: Which of these is a mental aspect of health?
options: Being free from stress and dullness
questions.explanation: A healthy mind is as important as a healthy body.

questions.text: Public health services also provide vaccines for children for ________.
options: Free or at very low cost
questions.explanation: Immunization is a key public health initiative of the government.

questions.text: Private services are ________ and concentrated in urban areas.
questions.explanation: Private players find it more profitable to set up in cities.

questions.text: The cost of treatment in a private hospital is ________.
questions.explanation: Patients often have to take loans to pay for private medical care.

questions.text: Safe drinking water is essential to prevent ________ diseases.
questions.explanation: Cholera and Typhoid are examples of diseases spread through contaminated water.

questions.text: The Kerala experience shows that ________ of funds can improve health.
questions.explanation: Giving power and money to local Panchayats helps address ground-level needs.

questions.text: Overall, healthcare in India is ________.
questions.explanation: While medical technology is advancing, access for the common man remains a major issue.
